extension.valueMarkdown: In Arizona she had primary care and cardiology follow-up that established the chronic disease baseline: diabetes requiring intensification, CKD emerging, atrial fibrillation managed with anticoagulation, and HFrEF treated medically. After moving to New Mexico she had an acute decompensated heart-failure hospitalization, then longitudinal follow-up with a heart-failure clinic, nephrology, and retina specialists for diabetic retinopathy with macular edema.

address.text: Lives in Albuquerque, New Mexico, after relocating from Phoenix, Arizona in early 2024 to be closer to her adult daughter and grandchildren.
Adult daughter in Albuquerque, New Mexico

coding.display: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (disorder)

code.text: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, NYHA class II to III

note.text: EF 35% with mild LV dilation, global hypokinesis, grade II diastolic dysfunction, mild left atrial enlargement, and exertional dyspnea consistent with NYHA class II to III symptoms.
Prior ambulatory monitor documented brief atrial fibrillation runs up to 140 bpm; same-day ECG showed sinus rhythm with premature atrial complexes.

reasonCode.text: HFrEF and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation follow-up

extension.valueMarkdown: Denise Walker saw Dr. Aaron Patel, MD, at Valley Heart Institute in Phoenix, Arizona for cardiology follow-up after workup earlier in 2022 had established heart failure with reduced ejection fraction and paroxysmal atrial fibrillation.
